'''Michael Korkidas''' ({{langx|el|Μιχαλης Κορκιδας}}; on 12 January 1981) is a former [[Greece national rugby league team|Greece]] international [[rugby league]] footballer who played as a {{rlp|pr}} in the [[National Rugby League|NRL]], the [[Super League]] and was captain of [[Greece national rugby league team|Greece]].

==Background==
Korkidas was born in [[Sydney, New South Wales, Australia]].

==Playing career==
===Early Career in Australia===
While attending [[Marist College Kogarah]], Korkidas played for the [[Australia national schoolboys rugby league team|Australian Schoolboys team]] in 1998.<ref>{{cite web|title=SportingPulse Homepage for Australian Secondary Schools Rugby League|url=http://www.sportingpulse.com/assoc_page.cgi?c=7-2130-0-0-0&sID=26424|accessdate=2008-10-10|publisher=SportingPulse}}</ref>

Korkidas made his professional rugby league début for the [[Sydney Roosters]] in the [[National Rugby League|NRL]] in Australia in 2001. He played two seasons at the club. At the end of the 2002 season, he joined up with [[Super League]]'s [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]].

===Super League Career (2003 – 2012)===
Korkidas played in the Super League from 2003 until 2012, playing for a number of clubs. These clubs are: [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]] (2003 – 2006), [[Salford Red Devils|Salford City Reds]] (2007 – 2008), [[Castleford Tigers]] (2008), [[Huddersfield Giants]] (2009) and returning to the [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]] in 2009 (until 2011) and finally, the [[Keighley Cougars]] (2012 – for one season).

He signed with [[Huddersfield Giants]] on a three-year contract from 2009 after negotiating a release from Castleford for his final season.<ref name="Korkidas released by Tigers">{{cite web|url=http://www.skysports.com/story/0,19528,12196_4227019,00.html|title=Korkidas released by Tigers|publisher=Sky Sports|date=2008-09-30|accessdate=2008-10-03}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|publisher=giantsrl.com|title=Korkidas joins Huddersfield Giants|url=http://www.rleague.com/db/article.php?id=31750|accessdate=2008-11-08|date=23 October 2008|url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20081023202430/http://www.rleague.com/db/article.php?id=31750|archivedate=23 October 2008}}</ref> However, in late May 2009, Korkidas returned to Wakefield mid-season in a deal with both clubs.<ref>[http://www.rleague.com/db/player/k/korkidas_michael/index.php Korkidas, Michael] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100103051256/http://rleague.com/db/player/k/korkidas_michael/index.php |date=3 January 2010 }}. rleague.com. Retrieved on 2012-03-14.</ref> He remained at the club until the end of the 2011 season.

In October 2011, he joined his old playing colleague Jason Demetriou, at the newly promoted Keighley Cougars for the 2012 season in the Championship.<ref>. Keighley Cougars. Retrieved on 2012-03-14.</ref> At the end of the season, he retired from the Super league, and returned to Australia. There is no mention of Korkidas returning to the NRL at this stage.

===Return to Australia (2013 – present)===
After his high-profile career in England, Korkidas returned to Australia in 2013 to ease off his playing career. He joined the [[Dapto Canaries]] club in 2013, a team that are playing in the [[Illawarra Rugby League]] competition. Korkidas finished his rugby league career with the club at the end of the 2014 season.

He also played for the [[Illawarra Cutters]] club in their final two games of the 2013 season. They participate in the [[NSW Cup]], the NRL's NSW feeder competition for the [[St George Illawarra Dragons]].

==Representative career==
Korkidas captained [[Greece national rugby league team|Greece]] in the team's first official international match in 2013, a 90–0 win over [[Hungary national rugby league team|Hungary]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Anasta Predicts Promising Future For Rugby League in Greece |url=https://europeanrugbyleague.com/articles/738/ireland-0-56-wales |website=European Rugby League |access-date=28 July 2022}}</ref>

Korkidas captained Greece for [[Thailand national rugby league team|Thailand's Chang 13]] first international match at King Mongkut's Institute of Technology, Ladkrabang, Bangkok, Thailand; Greece beat Thailand 90–0, 24-0 half time winning the Eurasia cup on 12 October 2013, although this was not a test-match.<ref>{{cite web|publisher=Rugby League Review|title=History made in Greece|url=http://www.rleague.com/db/article.php?id=26896|accessdate=2007-10-29|date=2006-10-29|url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20061115220946/http://www.rleague.com/db/article.php?id=26896|archivedate=15 November 2006}}</ref>
